quick_facts.label: Conducting authority
quick_facts.value: The All India Bar Examination (AIBE) is conducted under the Bar Council of India framework; the official AIBE portal is operated by the Bar Council of India Trust—PEARL FIRST.

quick_facts.label: Purpose
quick_facts.value: AIBE is a qualifying examination for advocates. The BCI states that practising advocates enrolled after 2010 must pass it in order to practise before courts.

quick_facts.label: Current published cycle
quick_facts.value: AIBE-XXI was held on June 7, 2026. Its result was declared on July 18, 2026.

quick_facts.label: Who may register
quick_facts.value: The published AIBE eligibility framework covers final-semester 3-year or 5-year LL.B. students without prior-semester backlogs, and specified graduates of BCI-recognised/approved institutions, including some who have not yet obtained a degree or State Bar Council enrolment certificate.

quick_facts.label: AIBE-XXI assessment and qualifying standard
quick_facts.value: The AIBE-XXI syllabus allocated 100 questions across 19 legal subjects. Although the announced thresholds were 45% for General/OBC and 40% for SC/ST and specially abled candidates, the BCI's July 18, 2026 release says the qualifying marks for that cycle were moderated downward by three marks.

quick_facts.label: Qualification outcome
quick_facts.value: BCI states that a candidate who clears AIBE is awarded a Certificate of Practice. Candidates qualifying under the undertaking-based framework may also need to submit their State Bar Council enrolment certificate within the applicable BCI timeline for processing.

sections.heading: What AIBE is for
paragraphs: AIBE is a professional qualifying examination in the pathway to legal practice in India, rather than an admission test for an LL.B. programme or an academic degree examination. The BCI describes it as a qualifying exam that advocates enrolled after 2010 must successfully pass in order to practise before courts.

The BCI says the examination was designed to establish a minimum benchmark for entry into legal practice and that, after clearing it, a candidate is awarded a Certificate of Practice. Its official AIBE information also distinguishes this purpose from non-practice career paths: the examination is directed at those intending to enter the legal profession.
bullets: For a law graduate planning litigation or advocate practice, AIBE is an important professional-regulation milestone after or alongside the relevant State Bar Council enrolment process.

It should not be treated as a substitute for an LL.B.; the official eligibility notices are specifically framed around 3-year and 5-year LL.B. candidates from BCI-recognised or BCI-approved institutions.

sections.heading: Eligibility: LL.B. status and institutional recognition matter
paragraphs: The current published AIBE portal clarification limits eligibility to candidates connected with a 3-year or 5-year LL.B. from a BCI-recognised university or BCI-approved college. It includes final-year/final-semester students with no earlier-semester backlogs, as well as eligible LL.B. pass-outs who may still be awaiting their degree, may not yet have enrolled with a State Bar Council, or may have surrendered an enrolment certificate.

The BCI eligibility notice expressly excludes candidates studying at or graduating from institutions that are not recognised or approved by the BCI. Candidates should therefore verify the status of their law institution and preserve the documentation required for their particular category before registering.
bullets: Final-semester students: no backlogs from previous semesters; the notice calls for self-attested semester marksheets.
Graduates awaiting degree conferral: eligible under the stated framework if they completed a qualifying 3-year or 5-year LL.B. at a recognised/approved institution.

Graduates without current State Bar Council enrolment, including certain candidates who surrendered enrolment because of employment: included in the published undertaking framework, subject to its conditions.

sections.heading: AIBE-XXI format and qualifying marks
paragraphs: For AIBE-XXI, the official syllabus specified 100 questions distributed across 19 subject areas. The largest allocations were Constitutional Law, Criminal Procedure Code/Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, and Civil Procedure Code at 10 questions each; the syllabus also expressly paired several legacy statutes with the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita and Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam.

The published AIBE-XXI schedule initially fixed the qualifying percentage at 45% for General and OBC candidates and 40% for SC/ST and specially abled candidates. However, the BCI's result release dated July 18, 2026 reports that the monitoring committee moderated the AIBE-XXI result by reducing the qualifying marks by three. Candidates should use the final result notice for their own cycle rather than assuming a prior cycle's cut-off will apply unchanged.
bullets: Core syllabus areas include constitutional law; criminal law and procedure; civil procedure; evidence; family law; professional ethics; contract, property and negotiable instruments; labour law; tax; torts; intellectual property; and cyber law.
AIBE-XXI was evaluated on a 100-mark basis, according to the BCI result release.

Exam rules and accommodations are cycle-specific. For AIBE-XXI, the official guidelines provided compensatory time for eligible candidates with more than 40% disability and set conditions for use of a scribe.

sections.heading: Application process: use the live official AIBE portal
paragraphs: Registration is conducted online through the official AIBE registration portal linked from the AIBE website. The AIBE-XXI cycle illustrates the typical sequence: online registration, online fee payment, a limited correction window, admit-card release, and the examination. Those dates are historical for AIBE-XXI and should not be reused for a future cycle without checking a new BCI notice.

For the AIBE-XXI cycle, registration opened February 11, 2026, closed April 30, 2026, online payment was due by May 1, corrections closed May 3, admit cards were scheduled from May 22, and the examination took place June 7, 2026. The official portal also warns that some application fields could not be edited after payment during the correction window.
bullets: Check the active notification before beginning: eligibility and documentation rules may be revised.
Prepare category-specific academic records and, where applicable, the undertaking and enrolment-related documents described in the official notice.
Download the admit card from the candidate portal and follow the examination-day requirements; AIBE-XXI required the admit card and government-issued photo identification at the centre.
Do not rely on historic registration dates or fees. AIBE-XXI registration and examination dates are already past as of July 2026.

sections.heading: Preparation strategy based on the official syllabus
paragraphs: Start with the official syllabus for the specific AIBE cycle, because subject labels and statutory references can change. For AIBE-XXI, the BCI allocated 100 questions across 19 areas, making a subject-weighted revision plan more defensible than studying only a handful of favourite law-school subjects.

Prioritise the high-weight areas first: constitutional law; criminal procedure and the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita; civil procedure; criminal law and the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita; evidence and the Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam; family law; and contract/property/negotiable instruments. Reserve dedicated revision time for professional ethics and cases of misconduct under BCI Rules, because this is a separately listed area of the official syllabus.
bullets: Create a checklist using the 19 official subject headings and their stated question weights.
Revise both the legacy and new criminal-law references where the official syllabus names both.

Practise accurate OMR completion and exam-day compliance: the AIBE-XXI instructions required blue or black ball-point pen markings and warned that incorrect question-booklet-set or roll-number details could lead to rejection of the answer sheet.
Read the current cycle's examination guidelines shortly before the test; authorised materials, timings, accessibility arrangements and centre procedures must be verified from that cycle's notice.

sections.heading: After qualifying: Certificate of Practice and enrolment follow-through
paragraphs: The BCI states that clearing AIBE leads to a Certificate of Practice and enables the qualified advocate to practise before courts, tribunals and administrative bodies in India. In practical career terms, this is the regulatory credential most directly relevant to entering litigation and advocate roles; it is separate from an employer's recruitment process for corporate, law-firm, PSU or other legal positions.

Candidates who qualified in AIBE-XIX or later under the undertaking-based eligibility framework must pay attention to the post-result enrolment-certificate process. The official AIBE portal stated on July 7, 2026 that the revised framework applied to those candidates and set January 6, 2027 as the then-published deadline to submit the enrolment certificate. This is a dated operational deadline, not a permanent rule, and candidates should confirm the latest BCI instruction before acting.
bullets: Check the result through the official AIBE candidate login rather than relying on third-party result pages.
Where a result is marked 'Pass (Undertaking)' or 'Withheld,' follow the official portal instructions for submitting the enrolment certificate.

Keep copies of the final result, enrolment documentation and Certificate of Practice records; official notices have clarified that provisional or draft online COP views are not themselves valid printable certificates.
